name: Power
description_short: Power Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving Southern Colorado with checking, savings, lending, and digital banking services across multiple locations.
description_long:
Power Credit Union operates as a federally insured credit union serving Southern Colorado communities, including recent expansion into Alamosa, Buena Vista, and Salida. The institution recently completed a system conversion welcoming members from Mountain River Credit Union, indicating ongoing growth and consolidation within the regional credit union network. As a member-owned cooperative, Power CU emphasizes personalized service from local staff alongside modern digital banking conveniences.

Power Credit Union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including multiple checking account tiers (Powered Up, Power, Empowered, Jump Start Teen, Premier 50+), personal and money market savings accounts, share certificates, and IRAs. On the lending side, they provide Visa credit cards (rates from 8.90% APR), personal loans and lines of credit, home mortgages, home equity loans (rates from 6.49% APR), auto loans with pre-approval options, RV loans, boat loans, and motorcycle loans. Digital services include 24/7 online and mobile banking, access to 5,000+ shared branches and 30,000+ shared ATMs, wire transfers, ACH early posting, and financial tools like credit score tracking and loan calculators.

Power Credit Union distinguishes itself through explicit emphasis on local interaction and personalized advocacy. Their marketing centers on staff expertise and member relationships rather than transactional efficiency alone. The organization provides specialized products like AutoSMART and Bankruptcy Auto Rebuilder Loans, indicating service to members with diverse credit profiles. They also offer Green Path Financial Wellness education and fraud awareness resources, plus tax filing assistance and community involvement programs.

As a credit union, Power CU operates under NCUA insurance rather than FDIC, meaning member deposits are protected up to $250,000 (or $500,000 for IRAs). Membership requirements and eligibility restrictions may apply, though the website does not specify these. Interest rates advertised are promotional minimums and actual rates depend on creditworthiness and loan terms. The recent Mountain River merger integration is ongoing, which could affect service consistency during the transition period.
services: Personal checking accounts (Powered Up, Power, Empowered, Jump Start Teen, Premier 50+)
Personal savings, money market, Christmas Club, and share certificates
Visa credit cards with no annual or balance transfer fees
Personal loans and lines of credit
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Auto loans with pre-approval and AutoSMART options
Online and mobile banking with 24/7 access
Shared branch and ATM network access (5,000+ branches, 30,000+ ATMs)
pros: Access to 5,000+ shared branches and 30,000+ shared ATMs provides extensive network beyond their own locations
Competitive rates advertised: Visa credit cards from 8.90% APR, home equity loans from 6.49% APR
Multiple specialized checking accounts designed for specific demographics (teens, seniors, high-balance members)
Bankruptcy Auto Rebuilder Loans available for members rebuilding credit after financial hardship
24/7 online and mobile banking with ACH early posting for faster fund availability
No annual fees on Visa credit cards and no balance transfer fees
Recent branch expansion to three new Colorado locations (Alamosa, Buena Vista, Salida)
cons: Recently integrated Mountain River Credit Union members into system; conversion integration ongoing may cause temporary service disruptions
Membership eligibility requirements not clearly disclosed on website; may restrict access compared to banks
Advertised rates are promotional minimums; actual APRs depend on creditworthiness, potentially limiting availability to best-qualified borrowers
Limited information on overdraft coverage options details, skip-a-pay terms, or holiday loan specifics
Credit union insurance (NCUA) covers up to $250,000 per account type, lower than some banking products
best_for: Southern Colorado residents seeking local credit union membership with personalized service
Members rebuilding credit after bankruptcy with access to specialized auto loans
Consumers wanting competitive rates on mortgages, home equity loans, and auto financing without annual credit card fees
diagnosis: Power Credit Union is best for Southern Colorado residents seeking a member-owned credit union alternative to traditional banks, particularly those wanting local staff interaction and competitive rates on mortgages and auto loans. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility may be restricted by employment or geographic affiliation, and the recent Mountain River integration means some service processes may still be stabilizing.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Power — Credit Union in Pueblo, CO | CreditDoc
meta_description: Power — NCUA-insured credit union in Pueblo, CO. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
